Description: Unique access point identifier. Sourced from each agency's stop ID field. Falls back to a stable coordinate-based ID (format: geom:<lat>,<lon> rounded to 5 decimal places, ~1 m precision) for records with missing or colliding IDs. Assigned during GDB curation; read as-is by Step 2.

Parent station ID. First populated by joining to the GTFS access_points layer (location_type = 2/3) on stop_id. Remaining nulls resolved by spatial buffer analysis at 150 ft / 300 ft / 500 ft / 1000 ft in Step 2. Manual corrections applied in Step 3 via the reviewed Excel workbook. All records are guaranteed to have a valid station_id before export.
Descriptive name of the pedestrian access point. Sourced from each agency's stop name field.

Highest-priority TOD tier for this access point. Derived from the tod_tier values of all stops sharing the same parent station — where stops of both tiers are present, Tier 1 takes precedence. Signals that an access point may serve stops of multiple tiers.
GTFS location type. Values: 2 = Entrance/Exit, 3 = Generic Node. Defaults to 2 if absent in the curated GDB layer. See https://gtfs.org/documentation/schedule/reference/#stopstxt for reference.
Pedestrian access point location in EPSG:4326. Used as the origin point for TOD zone buffer generation in Step 4.
